SEARCH
Package com.cellsimulation.stats

Class Statistics

java.lang.Object
com.cellsimulation.stats.Statistics
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public final class Statistics extends Object implements Serializable
Immutable snapshot of the population state at a given simulation tick.

A Statistics captures, for a single tick:

  • the tick number at which the snapshot was taken;
  • the count of persons currently in each DiseaseState;
  • the total population (sum of the four counts);
  • the average infection duration among infected persons;
  • the minimum and maximum immunity factor among living persons;
  • the distribution of infection durations among infected persons.

The class is intentionally immutable: once built, none of its fields can be modified, and getCountByState() exposes an unmodifiable view of the internal map. This makes it safe to share a snapshot across observers and threads without defensive copies.

Statistics is Serializable so that it can be stored in the history kept by StatisticsService and, if needed in the future, persisted to disk alongside other model objects.

See Also:

    Builds a snapshot of the given grid at the given tick.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    double
    getAverageInfectionDays()
    Returns the average number of infection days among the persons that are currently infected.
    int
    getCount(DiseaseState state)
    Returns the number of persons in the given state at the time of the snapshot.
    Map<DiseaseState,Integer>
    getCountByState()
    Returns the full count-by-state map.
    Map<Integer,Integer>
    getInfectionDaysHistogram()
    Returns the distribution of infection durations among the infected persons: each key is a number of infection days and the associated value is how many infected persons have been sick for exactly that many days.
    double
    getMaxImmunity()
    Returns the highest immunity factor found among the living persons (every state except DECEASED).
    double
    getMinImmunity()
    Returns the lowest immunity factor found among the living persons (every state except DECEASED).

  • Constructor Details

    • Statistics

      public Statistics(Grid grid, int tick)
      Builds a snapshot of the given grid at the given tick.

      The grid is traversed once in row-major order; every non-empty cell increments the counter of its current DiseaseState. The resulting map always contains an entry for each of the four states (SUSCEPTIBLE, INFECTED, RECOVERED, DECEASED), with a value of 0 when no person is in that state. The map is wrapped with Collections.unmodifiableMap(Map) to guarantee immutability.

      Parameters:
      grid - the grid to scan; must not be null
      tick - the tick number at which the snapshot is taken; must be greater than or equal to 0
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if grid is null or if tick is negative

  • Method Details

    • getTick

      public int getTick()
      Returns:
      the tick number at which this snapshot was taken

    • getCount

      public int getCount(DiseaseState state)
      Returns the number of persons in the given state at the time of the snapshot.
      Parameters:
      state - the disease state to look up
      Returns:
      the count for that state, or 0 if no entry exists (which should not happen, since the constructor populates all four states)

    • getTotalPopulation

      public int getTotalPopulation()
      Returns:
      the total number of persons present on the grid at the time of the snapshot (sum of the four state counts)

    • getCountByState

      public Map<DiseaseState,Integer> getCountByState()
      Returns the full count-by-state map.

      The returned map is already an unmodifiable view of the internal map; it is safe to share without further copying. It always contains an entry for each of the four DiseaseState values.

      Returns:
      an unmodifiable map from disease state to population count

    • getAverageInfectionDays

      public double getAverageInfectionDays()
      Returns the average number of infection days among the persons that are currently infected.
      Returns:
      the mean infection duration of infected persons, or 0.0 when no person is infected

    • getMinImmunity

      public double getMinImmunity()
      Returns the lowest immunity factor found among the living persons (every state except DECEASED).
      Returns:
      the minimum immunity factor, or 0.0 when no living person is present

    • getMaxImmunity

      public double getMaxImmunity()
      Returns the highest immunity factor found among the living persons (every state except DECEASED).
      Returns:
      the maximum immunity factor, or 0.0 when no living person is present

    • getInfectionDaysHistogram

      public Map<Integer,Integer> getInfectionDaysHistogram()
      Returns the distribution of infection durations among the infected persons: each key is a number of infection days and the associated value is how many infected persons have been sick for exactly that many days.

      The returned map is an unmodifiable view and only contains entries for durations that are actually present on the grid.

      Returns:
      an unmodifiable map from infection-day count to number of infected persons
